title: Konni Forest Division
tagline: Konni Forest Division — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: The Konni Forest Division is located in Kerala, India, and covers an area of about 331.66 square kilometres (128.05 sq mi).
overview.description: The Konni Forest Division is located in Kerala, India, and covers an area of about 331.66 square kilometres (128.05 sq mi). It is the first reserve forest in Kerala, which was declared on 9 October 1888 under the Travancore Forest Act of 1887. The division is part of the Western Ghats, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1 July 2012. The forest area is a repository of many endemic species of the Western Ghats.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Muringamangalam Sreemahadevar Temple
topPlaces.description: The Muringamangalam Sreemahadevar Temple, located half a kilometre from Konni Junction, is more than 1000 years old. It used to belong to the Pandalam Royal Family.

topPlaces.name: Chengara
topPlaces.description: Chengara is a small village located in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India. It is known for its natural environment, hills, and climate.

topPlaces.name: Konni, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Konni is a Grama Panchayat and Taluk headquarters in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India. Konni is known for its elephant cages, forests, and rubber plantations.

topPlaces.name: Vallicode-Kottayam
topPlaces.description: Vallicode-Kottayam is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. Vallicode-Kottayam village is a part of Konni Taluk, Pathanamthitta District of Kerala State, India.

topPlaces.name: Bethel Marthoma Church, Chengara
topPlaces.description: Bethel Marthoma Church is a Mar Thoma Syrian Church located at Chengara, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India. Bethel Marthoma Church belongs to the Ranni Nilackal Diocese of Mar Thoma Syrian Church.

topPlaces.name: Thannithode
topPlaces.description: Thannithode is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. It is near Konni town and is mainly a plantation township.

topPlaces.name: Aruvappulam
topPlaces.description: Aruvappulam is a remote village in the south eastern corner of Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. Aruvappulam is a grama panchayat (village council) within Konni Thaluk.

topPlaces.name: Kumbazha
topPlaces.description: Kumbazha is a main junction nestled within the heart of Pathanamthitta Municipality of Pathanamthitta Town, located in Kerala state, India. The serene Achenkovil river gracefully meanders through this historic locale.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Konni Forest Division?
faq.a: The best time to visit Konni Forest Division is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Konni Forest Division?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (84 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (83 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Konni Forest Division?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Konni Forest Division at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Konni Forest Division?
faq.a: Stays in Konni Forest Division start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Konni Forest Division?
faq.a: The top attractions are Muringamangalam Sreemahadevar Temple, Chengara, Konni, Kerala, Vallicode-Kottayam, Bethel Marthoma Church, Chengara.
